text:
Most of us are hoping that the next year will be a little lighter and more positive as 2020 comes to an end. It is possible to make a couple of simple resolutions that will keep you on track even when life gets messy. It is amazing how little changes in our daily lives can become huge changes in the long run.

The following are some of the simple New Year ideas that can help you in your personal development and well-being, particularly at a time when things still seem difficult. Positive thinking actually makes you wake up with more energy. Vaughn has attempted to keep everyone connected throughout the pandemic by organizing virtual events- yoga, dance classes, bootcamp workouts, movie nights, game nights, and a host of others.

Take a slow breath. We will go through some of the easy things that can make you calm, focused, and prepared to have a better year.

Make time just for yourself

You are already aware of the importance of maintaining a routine with online classes still occurring. School is important, but so is taking care of yourself. It is a wonderful idea to take a break by blocking out some of your me time. And seriously—it's not lazy. It's necessary. Try things like:

A brisk walk, jog, or house exercise.

Taking a mental break by watching your favorite show.

Listening to music you love

A quick 20-minute rest to recharge your energy.

Take care of your well-being

Everybody requires a bit of spoiling occasionally, and it does not need to be elaborate. It is as important to take care of your mind as it is to take care of your body. Here are a few easy ideas:

Read a page or two of an inspiring book.

Take a break off social media.

Switch off notifications to avoid being distracted all the time.

Take some silent time in meditation.

Do a puzzle or try a new hobby

Have paper around to doodle or sketch.

Record your ideas in a journal every day.

Such little practices can make you feel more balanced, healthier, and more grounded, which is consistent with the concepts of CFI and SRM (Kenny et al., 2015).
